name: Zen Mode
effect: Changes the Pokémon's form when its HP falls to half of its maximum or lower.

name: Wonder Skin
effect: Makes status moves used against the Pokémon more likely to miss.

name: Wonder Guard
effect: Blocks attacks that are not super effective against the Pokémon. Outside PvP battles, this protection is limited to absorbing two enemy hits; in PvP, that two-hit restriction does not apply.

name: Wind Rider
effect: Grants immunity to wind-based moves and raises the Pokémon's Attack when it absorbs one of these moves or when Tailwind is activated on its side of the field. Affected moves include Gust, Whirlwind, Blizzard, Icy Wind, Sandstorm, Twister, Heat Wave, Air Cutter, Tailwind, and Hurricane.

name: White Smoke
effect: Prevents the Pokémon's stats from being lowered during battle. When a Pokémon with this ability leads the party, the wild encounter rate is also reduced by 15%.

name: Weak Armor
effect: When the Pokémon is struck by a physical attack, its Defense is lowered and its Speed is increased.

name: Water Veil
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from being burned during battle.

name: Water Absorb
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a Water-type move, it restores HP instead of taking the move's normal damage.

name: Volt Absorb
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by an Electric-type move, it restores HP instead of suffering the move's normal effect.

name: Vital Spirit
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from falling asleep. Outside battle, placing a Pokémon with this ability at the front of the party increases the likelihood of encountering higher-level wild Pokémon.

name: Victory Star
effect: Increases the accuracy of the Pokémon and its allies.

name: Unnerve
effect: Prevents opposing Pokémon from consuming Berries while the Pokémon with this ability remains active in battle.

name: Unburden
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Speed after it uses or loses its held item. This Speed increase disappears immediately when the Pokémon leaves battle, including when it switches through moves such as U-turn or Volt Switch.

name: Unaware
effect: Causes the Pokémon to disregard stat changes during battle.

name: Truant
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from attacking on two consecutive turns, forcing it to remain inactive after a turn in which it attacks.

name: Trace
effect: When the Pokémon enters battle, it copies the ability of one of its opponents. The copied ability is temporary and is lost after the battle ends.

name: Torrent
effect: Increases the damage of the Pokémon's Water-type moves by 50% while its remaining HP is below 25% of its maximum. The boost ends if its HP rises above that threshold or if the Pokémon faints.

name: Thick Fat
effect: Reduces the damage the Pokémon receives from Fire-type and Ice-type moves by half.

name: Teravolt
effect: Allows the Pokémon's moves to ignore opposing abilities that would otherwise stop those moves from connecting or applying their effects.

name: Telepathy
effect: Protects the Pokémon from attacks used by its allies, allowing it to avoid friendly-fire moves in multi-Pokémon battles.

name: Technician
effect: Increases the power of moves with a base power of 60 or less by 50%.

name: Tangled Feet
effect: Raises the Pokémon's evasion while it is confused.

name: Synchronize
effect: When another Pokémon burns, poisons, or paralyzes the user, Synchronize attempts to inflict the same condition on that Pokémon; it does not reflect sleep or freeze and can activate through Substitute. Outside battle, an unfainted lead Pokémon with Synchronize gives wild Pokémon a 20% chance of having the same Nature, but this does not affect Alpha Pokémon.

name: Swift Swim
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed while rain is active.

name: Swarm
effect: Increases the power of Bug-type moves by 50% when the Pokémon has less than one-third of its maximum HP. When a Pokémon with Swarm leads the party, the wild encounter rate is increased by 10%.

name: Super Luck
effect: Raises the Pokémon's critical-hit ratio by one stage.

name: Suction Cups
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from being forced out of battle by switching moves. When it leads the party, it also increases the likelihood of getting a bite while fishing.

name: Sturdy
effect: If the Pokémon is at full health, an attack that would knock it out instead leaves it with 1 HP.

name: Storm Drain
effect: Draws Water-type moves toward the Pokémon and raises its Special Attack instead of allowing those moves to damage it.

name: Sticky Hold
effect: Prevents opposing Pokémon from stealing or removing the Pokémon's held item. When a Pokémon with Sticky Hold leads the party, it also increases the bite rate while fishing.

name: Stench
effect: Gives the Pokémon's attacks a chance to make their target flinch. When a Pokémon with Stench leads the party, the wild encounter rate is reduced by 15%.

name: Steadfast
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Speed after it flinches.

name: Static
effect: An opponent that makes physical contact with the Pokémon may become paralyzed. When a Pokémon with Static leads the party, encounters with Electric-type wild Pokémon become more likely.

name: Stall
effect: Makes the Pokémon act last within its move's normal priority bracket.

name: Speed Boost
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Speed by one stage at the end of each turn it remains in battle.

name: Soundproof
effect: Makes the Pokémon immune to moves classified as sound-based.

name: Solid Rock
effect: Reduces the damage received from super-effective attacks by 25%.

name: Solar Power
effect: During strong sunlight, the Pokémon's Special Attack is increased by 50%, but it loses one-eighth of its maximum HP at the end of each turn.

name: Snow Warning
effect: Creates a hailstorm when the Pokémon enters battle.

name: Snow Plow
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed while hail is active.

name: Snow Cloak
effect: Raises the Pokémon's evasion during hail and protects it from hail damage.

name: Sniper
effect: Causes the Pokémon's critical hits to inflict more damage than they normally would.

name: Slow Start
effect: Halves the Pokémon's Attack and Speed during its first five turns after entering battle.

name: Skill Link
effect: Causes moves that normally strike between two and five times to always hit the maximum number of times.

name: Simple
effect: Doubles the effect that stat-stage increases and decreases have on the Pokémon.

name: Shield Dust
effect: Prevents additional secondary effects from opposing damaging moves, such as flinching or stat reductions.

name: Shell Armor
effect: Prevents opposing attacks from landing critical hits on the Pokémon.

name: Sheer Force
effect: Increases the power of moves with additional effects by 30%, but removes those additional effects.

name: Shed Skin
effect: Gives the Pokémon a chance at the end of each turn to recover from a major status condition.

name: Sharpness
effect: Increases the power of moves classified as slicing moves.

name: Shadow Tag
effect: Prevents opposing Pokémon from switching or fleeing while the Pokémon with Shadow Tag remains active, although opposing Pokémon with Shadow Tag are not trapped by it.

name: Serene Grace
effect: Doubles the probability of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses a move.

name: Scrappy
effect: Allows the Pokémon's Normal- and Fighting-type moves to hit Ghost-type Pokémon and prevents Intimidate from lowering its Attack.

name: Sap Sipper
effect: Makes the Pokémon immune to Grass-type moves and raises its Attack by one stage when it is targeted by one.

name: Sand Veil
effect: Raises the Pokémon's evasion during a sandstorm and protects it from sandstorm damage.

name: Sand Stream
effect: Creates a sandstorm when the Pokémon enters battle.

name: Sand Rush
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed during a sandstorm and protects it from sandstorm damage.

name: Sand Force
effect: During a sandstorm, increases the power of the Pokémon's Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves and protects it from sandstorm damage.

name: Run Away
effect: Allows the Pokémon to escape successfully from ordinary wild battles regardless of the opposing Pokémon's Speed. It does not provide a direct benefit in trainer battles.

name: Rough Skin
effect: Damages an attacker whenever it uses a move that makes physical contact with the Pokémon.

name: Rock Head
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from receiving recoil damage from its own recoil-inflicting moves.

name: Rivalry
effect: Increases the damage dealt to Pokémon of the same gender but reduces the damage dealt to Pokémon of the opposite gender; it has no effect against genderless targets.

name: Regenerator
effect: Restores one-third of the Pokémon's maximum HP whenever it leaves battle by switching out.

name: Reckless
effect: Increases the power of moves that inflict recoil damage or cause crash damage if they miss.

name: Reactive Gas
effect: Suppresses the effects of other Pokémon's abilities while the Pokémon with Reactive Gas remains active on the battlefield.

name: Rattled
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Speed by one stage when it is hit by a Bug-, Ghost-, or Dark-type move or affected by Intimidate.

name: Rain Dish
effect: Restores one-sixteenth of the Pokémon's maximum HP at the end of each turn while rain is active.

name: Quick Feet
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Speed by 50% while it has a major status condition and prevents paralysis from applying its usual Speed reduction.

name: Pure Power
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Attack stat.

name: Protean
effect: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the type of the move it is about to use.

name: Pressure
effect: Causes opposing Pokémon to consume two PP instead of one when they use a move that targets the Pokémon. When a Pokémon with Pressure leads the party, higher-level wild Pokémon are more likely to appear.

name: Prankster
effect: Gives increased priority to the Pokémon's status moves.

name: Poison Touch
effect: Gives contact moves used by the Pokémon a chance to poison their target.

name: Poison Point
effect: An opponent that makes physical contact with the Pokémon may become poisoned.

name: Poison Heal
effect: Causes poison to restore the Pokémon's HP at the end of each turn instead of damaging it.

name: Plus
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Special Attack when an allied Pokémon with Plus or Minus is active alongside it.

name: Pickup
effect: Gives the Pokémon a chance to obtain an item after a battle if it is not already holding one. It may also collect an item consumed by another Pokémon during battle.

name: Pickpocket
effect: Steals an attacker's held item when the attacker makes physical contact, provided the Pokémon with Pickpocket is not already holding an item.

name: Own Tempo
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from becoming confused and stops Intimidate from lowering its Attack.

name: Overgrow
effect: Increases the power of the Pokémon's Grass-type moves by 50% when its remaining HP falls below 25% of its maximum.

name: Overcoat
effect: Protects the Pokémon from damage caused by weather and from powder- or spore-based moves.

name: Oblivious
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from becoming infatuated or being affected by Taunt and prevents Intimidate from lowering its Attack.

name: Adaptability
effect: Increases the same-type attack bonus applied to the Pokémon's moves from the usual 1.5× multiplier to 2×.

name: Aftermath
effect: Damages an attacker when its attack delivers the finishing blow to the Pokémon with Aftermath.

name: Analytic
effect: Increases the power of the Pokémon's move by 30% when it acts after its target during the turn.

name: Anger Point
effect: When the Pokémon is struck by a critical hit, its Attack is immediately raised to the maximum level of six positive stat stages.

name: Anticipation
effect: When the Pokémon enters battle, it shudders to alert its trainer if an opposing Pokémon knows a move that would be super effective against it.

name: Arena Trap
effect: Prevents opposing grounded Pokémon from switching out while the Pokémon with Arena Trap is active. Outside battle, placing it first in the party increases the wild encounter rate by 10%.

name: Battle Armor
effect: Protects the Pokémon from receiving critical hits during battle.

name: Big Pecks
effect: Prevents opposing attacks and effects from lowering the Pokémon's Defense stat.

name: Chlorophyll
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Speed while harsh sunlight is active.

name: Clear Body
effect: Prevents moves and abilities used by other Pokémon from lowering the bearer's stats. It does not stop stat reductions caused by the bearer's own moves, such as Superpower or Shell Smash.

name: Cloud Nine
effect: Suppresses every active weather effect while this Pokémon remains in battle, although it does not remove the weather itself or stop its remaining duration from progressing.

name: Color Change
effect: After the Pokémon is hit by a move, its type changes to match the type of that move.

name: Competitive
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Special Attack by two stages whenever an opponent lowers one of its stats. It does not activate from self-inflicted or allied stat reductions, and it activates separately for every stat lowered.

name: Compoundeyes
effect: Boosts the Pokémon's accuracy during battle. The wiki does not list an additional overworld effect for this ability.

name: Contrary
effect: Reverses stat-stage changes affecting the Pokémon, so increases become decreases and decreases become increases.

name: Cursed Body
effect: May disable a move that is used against the Pokémon.

name: Cute Charm
effect: When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 15% chance of becoming infatuated, provided the two Pokémon have compatible genders and the attacker is not protected by Oblivious. An infatuated Pokémon has a 50% chance of being unable to attack each turn. When this ability leads the party, encounters with Pokémon of the opposite gender increase by a flat 15%, except for Alpha Pokémon.

name: Damp
effect: Prevents opposing Pokémon from using moves that cause the user to self-destruct.

name: Defeatist
effect: Lowers the Pokémon's offensive stats when its remaining HP falls to half of its maximum or less. The wiki's description does not specify the exact reduction multiplier.

name: Defiant
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Attack when one of its stats is lowered. The wiki's page does not specify the exact number of stages gained.

name: Download
effect: When the Pokémon enters battle, it raises either Attack or Special Attack according to which defensive stat is lower among its opponents.

name: Drizzle
effect: Causes rain to begin when the Pokémon enters battle.

name: Drought
effect: Causes sunny weather to begin when the Pokémon enters battle.

name: Dry Skin
effect: Makes the Pokémon lose HP during sunny weather, while rain and Water-type attacks restore its HP instead. The wiki's page does not specify the exact healing or damage percentages.

name: Early Bird
effect: Halves the amount of time the Pokémon remains asleep in battle, allowing it to wake up sooner than normal.

name: Effect Spore
effect: When the Pokémon is struck by a contact move, there is a chance that the attacker will be poisoned, paralyzed, or put to sleep.

name: Filter
effect: Reduces the damage the Pokémon receives from super-effective attacks. The wiki's page does not state the exact reduction percentage.

name: Flame Body
effect: Burns an attacker when it hits the Pokémon with a contact move. When a Pokémon with Flame Body occupies the first party slot, Eggs also require less time to hatch.

name: Flare Boost
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Special Attack while it is burned.

name: Flash Fire
effect: Makes the Pokémon immune to Fire-type moves. After it is targeted by one, its own Fire-type attacks are strengthened.

name: Flower Gift
effect: Strengthens the Pokémon's party while sunny weather is active. The wiki's page does not identify which stats are increased or the exact multiplier.

name: Forecast
effect: Changes Castform's form according to the weather currently active in battle. This ability is exclusive to Castform.

name: Forewarn
effect: When the Pokémon enters battle, it reveals the opposing Pokémon's move with the highest base power.

name: Friend Guard
effect: Reduces the damage received by the Pokémon's allies while the bearer is active in battle. It has no effect outside battle.

name: Frisk
effect: Reveals whether an opposing Pokémon is holding an item, but does not identify the item itself. In horde battles, it gives repeated notifications when multiple opponents hold items, without indicating which opponent holds each one.

name: Gluttony
effect: Causes held Berries that normally activate at low HP to be consumed when the Pokémon reaches half of its maximum HP instead.

name: Guts
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Attack by 50% while it has a major status condition and prevents a burn from applying its usual Attack reduction.

name: Harvest
effect: Gives the Pokémon a chance to recreate a Berry after that Berry has been consumed during battle.

name: Healer
effect: Gives the Pokémon a chance to cure a status condition affecting one of its allies.

name: Heavy Metal
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's effective weight during battle.

name: Hyper Cutter
effect: Prevents other Pokémon from lowering the bearer's Attack stat. It does not state that self-inflicted Attack reductions are prevented.

name: Illuminate
effect: Prevents the Pokémon's accuracy from being lowered and allows its moves to ignore the target's evasion changes. Outside battle, placing it first in the party increases the wild encounter rate by 10%.

name: Intimidate
effect: Lowers each opponent's Attack by one stage when the Pokémon enters battle. Outside battle, placing it first in the party repels some lower-level wild Pokémon.

name: Honey Gather
effect: After winning a battle, a Pokémon with this ability may obtain Honey as its held item, provided it is in the first party slot and is not already holding another item.

name: Huge Power
effect: Doubles the Pokémon's Attack stat.

name: Hustle
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Attack but reduces its accuracy. Outside battle, placing a Pokémon with Hustle first in the party makes higher-level wild Pokémon more likely to appear.

name: Ice Body
effect: Gradually restores the Pokémon's HP while a hailstorm is active.

name: Insomnia
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from being put to sleep during battle. Moves that require the user to fall asleep, such as Rest, fail when used by a Pokémon with this ability.

name: Iron Fist
effect: Increases the damage of punching moves by 20%.

name: Keen Eye
effect: Prevents other Pokémon from lowering the bearer's accuracy. Outside battle, placing a Pokémon with Keen Eye first in the party repels some lower-level wild Pokémon.

name: Immunity
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from being poisoned during battle.

name: Infiltrator
effect: Allows the Pokémon's attacks to bypass defensive barriers and Substitute when striking a target.

name: Hydration
effect: Cures the Pokémon of status conditions while rain is active.

name: Imposter
effect: Automatically transforms the Pokémon into the opposing Pokémon when it enters battle.

name: Iron Barbs
effect: Damages an opposing Pokémon whenever it makes physical contact with the Pokémon that has this ability.

name: Justified
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Attack by one stage each time it is hit by a Dark-type move.

name: Klutz
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from using the effects of its held item.

name: Illusion
effect: Allows the Pokémon to disguise itself as another Pokémon during battle. The retrievable wiki text does not provide more detailed mechanics about how the disguise is selected or broken.

name: Leaf Guard
effect: Protects the Pokémon from being afflicted with status conditions while sunny weather is active.

name: Levitate
effect: Makes the Pokémon immune to damaging Ground-type moves and protects it from Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Arena Trap. These protections are disabled during Gravity or when the Pokémon is grounded by an Iron Ball, Ingrain, or Smack Down.

name: Lightningrod
effect: Makes the Pokémon immune to Electric-type attacks and raises its Special Attack when it is targeted by one.

name: Limber
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from becoming paralyzed.

name: Liquid Ooze
effect: Causes opponents using HP-draining moves against the Pokémon to take damage instead of recovering HP normally.

name: Magma Armor
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from being frozen. When a Pokémon with Magma Armor is placed first in the party, it also reduces the time required for Eggs to hatch.

name: Magnet Pull
effect: Prevents opposing Steel-type Pokémon from switching out while the Pokémon with this ability remains active.

name: Marvel Scale
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Defense by 50% while it is affected by a status condition.

name: Minus
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Special Attack when another active Pokémon has either Plus or Minus.

name: Mold Breaker
effect: Allows the Pokémon's moves to ignore opposing abilities that would otherwise cause those moves to fail.

name: Moody
effect: At the end of each turn, randomly raises one of the Pokémon's stats by two stages and lowers a different stat by one stage. It has no effect outside battle.

name: Motor Drive
effect: Makes the Pokémon immune to damaging Electric-type moves and raises its Speed when it is hit by one.

name: Moxie
effect: Raises the Pokémon's Attack after it knocks out another Pokémon.

name: Multiscale
effect: Reduces the damage received from an attack by half when the Pokémon is at full HP. It has no effect outside battle.

name: Multitype
effect: Changes Arceus's type to correspond to the Plate it is holding. The wiki identifies this as an ability exclusive to Arceus.

name: Natural Cure
effect: Removes the Pokémon's status condition whenever it switches out during battle.

name: Mummy
effect: Replaces the ability of a Pokémon that makes physical contact with the bearer, giving the attacker Mummy. The recoverable wiki text confirms this interaction through Cofagrigus, although the dedicated ability page itself was not retrievable.

name: Inner Focus
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from flinching. However, moves or abilities that ignore other abilities can bypass this protection.

name: No Guard
effect: Ensures that moves used by the Pokémon and moves directed against it successfully connect. Outside battle, placing a Pokémon with No Guard first in the party also improves the effectiveness of lure encounters.

name: Heatproof
effect: Halves the residual damage the Pokémon takes from burn, reducing the HP loss to 1/32 of its maximum HP per turn instead of the usual 1/16. This was confirmed on the wiki's general Status Effect page rather than a dedicated Heatproof page, so any additional effects (e.g. reduced Fire-type damage taken, as in the official games) are not confirmed here.

name: Magic Bounce
effect: Reflects certain effects back at the Pokémon that caused them, confirmed specifically for entry hazards — Spikes, Stealth Rock, Sticky Web, and Toxic Spikes. These hazards can only be reflected once per side, by the leftmost Pokémon affected by Magic Bounce or Magic Coat. This was confirmed on the wiki's Magic Coat (move) page rather than a dedicated Magic Bounce page, so whether it also reflects other status moves (as in the official games) is not confirmed here.

name: Magic Guard
effect: Prevents the Pokémon from losing HP to burn damage, though burn's Attack reduction still applies. This was confirmed on the wiki's general Status Effect page rather than a dedicated Magic Guard page, so its full list of protected indirect-damage sources (e.g. Sandstorm, Life Orb, entry hazards, as in the official games) is not confirmed here.

name: Light Metal
effect: Halves the Pokémon's effective weight in battle.

name: Normalize
effect: Changes all of the Pokémon's moves to Normal type.

name: Tinted Lens
effect: Increases the power of the Pokémon's 'not very effective' moves.

name: Blaze
effect: Increases the power of the Pokémon's Fire-type moves by 50% when its remaining HP falls below 25% of its maximum. The boost ends if its HP rises above that threshold or if the Pokémon faints. Exclusive to Fire-type starter Pokémon.

name: Air Lock
effect: Suppresses every active weather effect while this Pokémon remains in battle, although it does not remove the weather itself or stop its remaining duration from progressing. Functionally identical to Cloud Nine. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (sin página localizable) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 — sin página localizable en PokeMMO Wiki tras múltiples variantes de URL, extraido 2026-07-10

name: Bad Dreams
effect: Damages a sleeping opponent for 1/8 of its maximum HP at the end of each turn while it remains asleep. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (sin página localizable) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 — sin página localizable en PokeMMO Wiki tras múltiples variantes de URL, extraido 2026-07-10

name: Toxic Boost
effect: Increases the Pokémon's Attack by 50% while it is poisoned. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (sin página localizable) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 — sin página localizable en PokeMMO Wiki tras múltiples variantes de URL, extraido 2026-07-10

name: Turboblaze
effect: Allows the Pokémon's moves to ignore opposing abilities that would otherwise stop those moves from connecting or applying their effects. Functionally identical to Teravolt. (sin verificar contra PokeMMO Wiki (sin página localizable) — dato de Pokémon oficial Gen 5, por instrucción de Ferran)
source: Pokémon oficial Gen 5 — sin página localizable en PokeMMO Wiki tras múltiples variantes de URL, extraido 2026-07-10
